boundary_invariants: public semantic precedence != private institutional applicability
public RFC accepted != private sovereign implementation disclosed or verified
private dependency review receipt present != public verification of full private implementation
a private applicability decision may restrict or decline adoption but must not silently rewrite public Semantic Core semantics
missing private input is recorded or blocked and is never guessed

rules: a lower-precedence artifact cannot silently redefine a higher-precedence semantic owner
runtime behavior does not become normative through existence
a test may reveal contradiction but cannot resolve institutional Authority
no last-write-wins or newest-file-wins rule is permitted

permitted_scope: whether an institution adopts a public RFC or profile
where an institution adopts a public RFC or profile
under whose private Authority an institution adopts a public RFC or profile
which private restrictions govern institutional adoption
prohibited_scope: silently rewrite public RFC canonical semantics
silently weaken a public sovereign invariant
represent institutional adoption as public semantic ownership
represent non-adoption as a change to public RFC meaning
private_institutional_applicability.public_claim_boundary: private doctrine may determine institutional applicability, but public semantic meaning remains owned by the accepted public RFC abstraction

conflict_resolution_behavior.missing_private_input_behavior: retain an abstract reference or enter blocked_pending_private_l0_input
conflict_resolution_behavior.authority_boundary: a public test, schema, validator, runtime, repository actor, or model cannot resolve private institutional Authority

public_projection_rules.private_dependency_projection: only a bounded public-safe receipt or abstract unresolved reference
public_projection_rules.evidence_projection_rule: reference the restricted evidence location without disclosing restricted evidence contents
public_projection_rules.authority_projection_rule: use an abstract public role or opaque verifiable reference without assigning or exposing a private role identity
public_projection_rules.claim_rule: a public projection must state its known limitations and must not claim full private implementation verification

claim_boundary: receipt proves only the declared bounded review result
receipt does not prove full private implementation
private_dependency_review_receipt_contra.reviewed_private_dependency_classes_rule: only abstract dependency classes may be listed
private_dependency_review_receipt_contra.reviewer_authority_reference_rule: must be an abstract public role or opaque verifiable reference and must not expose a restricted identity
private_dependency_review_receipt_contra.evidence_location_reference_rule: must identify the bounded evidence location without disclosing restricted evidence contents
private_dependency_review_receipt_contra.public_safe_restrictions_rule: must include only restrictions safe for public projection
private_dependency_review_receipt_contra.known_limitations_rule: must state every known public claim limitation

not_evaluable_behavior.unknown_private_semantics_rule: do not ignore, infer, guess, or map to the nearest permissive behavior
not_evaluable_behavior.public_claim_rule: state that private implementation was not fully verified

revocation_and_supersession_behavior.supersession_rule: a newer receipt does not silently erase or rewrite the prior receipt
revocation_and_supersession_behavior.claim_rule: a revoked or superseded receipt remains historically resolvable and cannot be used outside its original validity interval
